title: senecio pseudocrispus
intro: Senecio pseudocrispus, also known as annual burnet-sage or prickly ragwort, is a plant species within the sunflower family that is native to South Africa. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of pyrrolizidine alkaloids. Consuming Senecio pseudocrispus can help alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma by loosening mucus and reducing inflammation, while also providing relief from menstrual cramps and other symptoms of PMS. Senecio pseudocrispus can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
